text: I was trying to get my phone unlocked and switch to Verizon. I got the phone from my boyfriends dad and there wound up being some kind of a charge issue on the account. I wound up calling and talking to two or three of the customer service people and went into the store even to try to figure out what was going on. It wasn't until I went into the store to get it unlocked that we discovered the charge. All the worker did was temporarily unlock it. So when I got back home I called again and talked to Matt in the KC store. He was so helpful and very patient. He's the best one I talked to.
Unreliable and inconsistent service. Always some lame excuse. F#ck T Mobile. Scum bag thieves. Lots of failed texts, dropped calls, no service, etc... How are these pukes still in business ?

TMobile is the worst company I have ever dealt with their customer service sucks and they will not unlock my phone even though I paid cash for it I've been going at them for a week do not ever go to T-Mobile stay clear their service sucks TMobile sucks don't waste your money

I cannot believe how horrible the customer service is. They sent my bill to collections after two days! They wouldn’t allow me to use my account to pay it because they said I needed my Tmobile pin (this was their horrible staff in the Philippines). Within three days the bill went from $88 to $110. I’m so sickened by this company. Not to mention the awful call centre in the Philippines where no one knows what they are doing. The company promises all of these things and does not deliver. By far and above, my WORST experience with a cell phone company. NEVER AGAIN. The CEO should be ashamed of the state they have let it get into.

TMobile is stealing from customers bank accounts. They will charge you more than once for your bill. My bill was 50 dollars I was charged 159.00 dollars they then took the 53.00 dollars out and  put the rest as a credit and said they could not find the payments.The money has mysteriously vanished from my bank account and sitting as a credit with them. Boycott TMobile before your money is missing.

Poor quality at best the worst disservice to the country next to the current President if you try to track your replacement phone they gamestop you poorer Quality on top of that they have the same stupid questions to sidestep the fact they do nothing but make excuses if you are charging money to use a device i better have it not as though i need you holding me up before work only to give you another nickel in your pocket if you can't fulfill your job don't keep me from doing mine and don't use this company as a brand Can't seem to rest my password why not get rerouted on eight 23 minute conversations before i must rush in it's just more T-mobile nonsense..............................don't contact me don't try to enquire about my opinion my opinion is that of many others get tech better help condolences for your bad quality of service

I haven't had service with Tmobile in 2 months. I'm due a refund but have not received it as if yet. Before you try this company (and I use that word loosely) try somewhere else. Use a can and string. It's better than tmobile.

Please read! 
Horrible company! 
I was told by corporate that they bought Sprint, who I have been with over 10 years.  That Sprint was gong out of business and I needed to switch. I go into the store in Gallatin, TN. Which no one knew what they were doing. I was told it would be no problem, no fees and no hassle. Well, I should have walked away. They hooked up one line with the others to be hooked up in two days.  We went back in two days later and they wanted us to pay $400+. So, I asked to close everything and return my phone. They wouldn’t return my phone and told my husband they didn’t have it but, I had just unlocked it 20 mins earlier. Robert (manager) told my husband to go to Sprint as soon as possible switch back to them and call him so, he could close the account. Which we went to Sprint and my husband called Robert back and he wasn’t there. The manager at Sprint had told us they should have given the phone back to me.  We had to wait until Monday. We call Robert on Monday and he tells my husband that he would have to call customer service to close account. Another lie! We called corporate to see if we could get my phone back and close the account. They wouldn’t return my phone and had a hard time trying to close the account. They kept trying to get us to say with them. Why would we do that we don’t have any of there phones and honestly the whole experience was horrible! I wasted over two days of my time dealing with all of this.  We also, will have a bill from T-mobile and Robert says he would take care of it when it comes in. We will see if that’s a lie too! Update soon to come!
T mobile good when you feel like getting your money stolen with charges they pull out they a*s No cap

This has been my worst experience with T-mobile. Since day one they never got my plan bill right. And I specifically got a plan with all tax and fees included to not deal with changes and is always changing. I've been dealing with the same issue for 6 months already talking with my rep and customer service and seems like they are never on the same page. Even a mistake that they made, I'm still paying for that. They will not fix it. The policy that T-mobile have is making me pay for a mistake I never did. I'm just trying to find a way to get out of this company. Never recommending this.

I switched from Verizon to T Mobile and that was a terrible mistake.  I have such terrible service that the majority of my phone calls drop.  I will be in major metropolitan areas such as Los Angeles or San Francisco and don’t have good enough service to make a call. And if I am in an office I can’t take a single call unless I’m standing at the window. My cellular data is pretty terrible too. I can barely open web pages. My bars jump up and down drastically and my phone is constantly searching for a signal. I have an iPhone that is less than 2 years old and when I called T Mobile their response was for me to buy a new phone.

I DID KNOW THAT T-MOBILE HAS SO MANY BAD REVIEWS. THIS COMPANY IS A RIP, STEAL YOUR MONEY SLOW BY SLOW. CHECK  BILL very well. I WAS ON AUTOMATIC PAYMENT WHICH ALMOST NEVER CHECK MY ACCOUNT. WASTING MONEY AND TIME TALKING TO REPRESENTATIVES.

I was looking for a cheaper plan and decided to call the customer service. The representative asked me if there is anyone in my family who is older than 55 years old. I said “yes, there is”. He then offered me the unlimited 55+ Plan. I gladly accepted it. He told me that he changed the plan and I was all set and there was no further action needed. After a while I received a text message from t mobile saying that they need my mom to bring her ID to a t mobile store to verify her age. (The representative I had spoken to on the phone didn’t tell me anything about that).  So we went to the store and were told that we actually needed to make my mom the primary holder of the account in order to qualify for the 55+ plan. In order to do that we had to call the customer service again (which I had not been told by the representative that I initially had spoken to on the phone) . SO WE EVENTUALLY DID CALL THE CUSTOMER SERVICE trying to simply make my mom (63 years old) the account holder so we could qualify for the 55+ Service plan.  They literally kept us on the phone for 2 hours , one of the representatives was unbelievably rude, and eventually hung up on me. Long story short, I still haven’t been able to change the account holder. Besides, they keep sending us text messages and emails saying that they still need to verify my mom’s age for us to qualify for the 55+ plan, even though we have already provided to them all the documents they needed for verification. Like, seriously, why does it have to take so much pain in the **** to simply change the account holder. Whenever I call the customer service it always sounds like there are re***ded people working there.  
They’re absolutely inexperienced, incompetent and don’t seem to have any idea about what they’re doing.. they tell you one thing and in reality it turns out to be another. It’s truly outrageous. 
The management really needs to give their employees a proper training..... It’s truly outrageous. 
That is not the only bad experience I’ve had with them. There’s so many I could write a book. It’s just that this one is the most recent and most frustrating one. I am really considering switching to another company.

I decided to switch from Verizon to T-Mobile, they told me they would pay my FINAL bill from Verizon. When I paid the bill i tried to send it to T-Mobile It DIDN't work so I called them. 
The guy told me "that is wrong, we DO NOT PAY for the final bill."
THIS IS FALSE ADVERTISING to persuade people to switch. My relationship with T-Mobile is done, I cannot work with a company that LIES to its customers.

WHERE do I start? . . if there are negative stars or minuses - yes, put me under both - T Mobile SUCKS! - I signed over from AT& T because they were nickel and diming me, but I guess you pay for what and you'll get  - so, for the first month in I had NO service and was charged, with that being said - let's fast forward to buying insurance( via Assurant/ Jump/ Apple Care) - which I have purchased, for the past 5 years - with that said, just a month ago, I signed up into a family plan (for the free Netflix - and to cut the overall cost - by $20.) and one of the customer service reps. (Cianna, from VA - with wkend scheduled between 9:20-8:50 PM) made the transition "seemed" so smooth and thought everything was good - TIL - a few days ago when my phone broke and found out THIS representative took out my insurance and now I have to pay for a whole NEW phone, and speaking with a supervisor (Mica), the only thing she could do, was to offer a $50. credit - so, let's do the math shall we, that's $15. for 5 years = $900. dollars, which customer service rep. Cianna stole from me - it might not be in a stick up in the ghetto but this is the same - and all I'm getting is a $50. credit and having to pay out of pocket for a new phone - PLEASE if someone else knows where and who else to post this to to get this rep to pay for my new phone that would be great!

I'd give 0 stars if this site allowed me to do so, so I can write. but I give 2 stars because I had no problems really. up until last year when my supposed private number was accumulating calls of spam, at least I give credit to the phone 5s for alerting me of "scam likely" calls incoming.  But then when it got down to the kill where I kept getting strange numbers calling me, of course, I never answered, and these calls ever since I switched to a new number(it only cost me $10 over 4 years ago, but when I wanted to change my number, they told me it will cost me another $20! that should be on them for all these strange foreign numbers coming in with the same first 6 digits as mine!! (551-666) and a different 4 digit extension every time they called!!  I find out it is a foreign ISIS-like person cause he spoke NO ENGLISH whatsoever!! I had to call him back and tell him to stop calling me, and he returns with a text saying "Fck you" and etc. I had to ask who it was, he said he was 23, from NY and Egyptian?? and said he was from Uber when I googled his number 551-6661492 it said this is an UBER number. and this jerk was saying he was coming to get me to take me out for a few drinks?? yeah right, GTFO!!.  OMG. right there that tells you instantly--danger!!   How did this assnut get my number in the first place?? and I find out one of the options I had INCESSANTLY used "Block the number" , well, this feature WAS NOT BLOCKING THE F-ING NUMBER!! because I called that number back and it was the same assnut who kept calling my phone!! and I got real angry and incensed, threw my phone at my wall in my house and it breaks, lol. and it was already starting to die because the batteries on these phones suck!!!  and it was already coming apart so I dismantled it, destroyed it, especially my SIM card, lol. and chucked in the trash--where it belongs!!  canceled my service cause I could never get any service when I was out of state!! among other reason than the one I just mentioned, dysfunctional "block the number" features. and this company is just suspiciously dysfunctional as it is. This company sucks to high hell!! and I'm nver going back again!!

They should only get “0” stars. This is a sorry company. They made me pay for a service pin so I could activate a SIM card. Then for a technical reason couldn’t activate the sim and then told me me service pin was non refundable. they stole my money!

I switched over to T-Mobile from AT&T and had 3 lines transported, T-Mobile said they would give me 3 pre paid cards for switching over and I only received one. Called them and they declined my other two cards, this is not right. I hope someone can read this and help me out because they lied to me and it’s been a year and I still haven’t received the other two which is over $1000, please help

This is the poorest store experience I've ever had. It would be dangerous to use T Mobile if you ever need your phone for any kind of emergency service. T Mobile service would might not be reliable if your child was in an accident or your parent or grandparent had a cardiac event. I recommend any other reliable service such as Verizon and most other service and product providers. The Reston, VA 1837 Fountain Drive store is only available to sell products. The manager at that store, Rafi, is unable to do the simplest maintenance or provide even the simplest information on T Mobile services or products. Not only is there no customer service, the products are overpriced and of very poor quality. Three or four people work in the store but only Zee is able to answer even very simple questions.

Before signing any agreement with T-Mobile, make sure you are aware that should you decide to change carriers, they will lock you phone. Doesn't matter if your phone is paid for. Doesn't matter if your account is current. Should you decide to change carriers, understand that it may take several days before they decide you can use your phone again. Evidently, if you've ever used their service, they think they own your phone. Should you call their customer service, they'll put you on the phone with an arrogant condescending jerk. My advice is to think twice. There are plenty of other options available.

I been customer with tmobile for last 4 years and buy two phone and made extra payment  for 3 month for my phone and last three month they was giving my credit and  but i told them i dint need credit some one stole my money i made extra payment and they beeing smort and guys i talk to his name josh from taxes  area and he was very bad and talk very roud  to me and im getting my paper togather and i think take it to store witch employee stole my money   and not post it my account be carefulm with tmobile im thinking to switch from tmobile

If I could go zero stars, I would! Took them death certificate of wife back on June '18 to have account transferred to my name. Said they would and lower the bill w her line shut off. They didn't transfer not lower the bill but DID shut off line. Been fighting w them for over 9 mths to transfer phone. FINALLY lower bill last mth yet STILL won't/haven't transferred to my name. IM DONE W THEM AND THEIR CRAP

I only had T-Mobile for 4 and half months. The coverage was the worst I have ever experienced. I received different information from everyone I spoke to in customer service(exception was Stacy). One rep would give me information and the next would tell me the previous rep was wrong. It got to be ridiculous. I paid for the last 30 days and I left before the 30 days was used up. I was told I would get a credit for the unused days by a rep. I did not get a credit, instead I was double charged for 9 days and then when they credited me for the double charge, they said you got your credit. I said "no" I received a credit for the double charging you did, but not the credit I should have received from my original monthly charge. NO ONE could understand that, I finally gave up. They will never see business from me again and I would never recommend T-Mobile to anyone. They owe me money!!!!!!

My name is patricio i been a customer almost for  4 years 04/8/2018 2 days ago i upgrade my lg stylo 2 plus to a lg 4 stylo has some problems and i went to  brandon mall t mobile in florida  to return the cell and upgrade to a s8 o i8 but the store manager charger me $50 restock fee on a cell that was broken from the begi nning so i ask her  to waive the restock fee so since the cell i got was defected from the box and so i can get the upgrade and she refuse and did not even try to work something out ,so i can stay with t mobile as a customer btw i was paying $137 a month for almost 4 years  and i kno i am a little fish in the ocean but have to.put this out there .and i m.posting it on all social media ,i cannt believed for $50 she excuse me t mobile is not getting my $137.00 a month

The worst customer service I have ever spoken to. I call to make a 3 day extension to pay my bill. She first asks what is my 6 number pin and my pin never works when I call supposedly so I always have to change it and they wonder why it doesn't work. Then she tells me its an $8 charge to make an extension and I have to give her a credit card number to make sure I pay in 3 days. I felt my blood boiling when I've been a customer for over 6 years and paid on time every month and this is how u treat a responsible customer who is telling T mobile when I can make a payment and they dont trust me after 6 years of being loyal and on time patments. This is an absolute joke. So if I don't have a credit card number to give you then u can't give me a 3 day extension...haha...This customer service sucks...just look at all these reviews. At the end she couldn't do anything for me flat out told me that...what kind of customer service is that?...No solution and can give 2 s*!#$ how they treat hard working loyal customers...I'm looking for a new carrier after that phone call.

I used to work for over 10 years in the call center in south Texas.  I can say for a fact that most people in upper management lack leadership and cover each others back when they do something wrong. Some recruiters, supervisors,  senior reps and managers try to sleep with  attractive girls that are new hires. I know it happens because I've had conversations with 4 girls that went through it. In one occasion one manager called an employee's cell phone to try to make a move on her. She told me her story one day we were having lunch. And if they don't like you or you know too much they start finding ways to get rid off employees. The work environment in that particular call center was toxic.  So with management like that don't expect the best treatment.

WARNING JUMP SCAM there is 2 option which T-mobile sale rep fail to warn customers 1 option is renting the phone for 18 months and after the 18 months you must return the phone or purchase the phone. Option 2 payment installment for 24 months. So be careful not to fall into this trap as I did.

I have been a customer of T-Mobile for nearly twenty years. The customer service was never great, but when I switched to Sprint to take advantage of the free year I learned what truly bad customer service looks like. I was originally told my phone was unlocked, but after porting to Sprint it turned out it wasn't. I called T-Mobile repeatedly and over the course of two days, with a non-working phone, I was given conflicting information, misinformation and in the end it will likely be a full four days until my phone is unlocked.  To add insult to injury they are trying to bill me for the full month, even though I only used their service for 10 days of the billing cycle. With other carriers offering great deals, better than T-Mobile, there is no reason to settle for appalling service. Do yourself a favor and look elsewhere for your phone service provider.

If I could put zero stars here I would. So, my dad likes family plan packages but didn't want to pay the high price of Verizon any longer even though it was a pretty decent carrier. We went to the Rochester, MI. store together and was told that they could not guarantee that their network would work with our phones from Verizon. My dad bought one from them but that was part of their deal so he could save money. I am a Lyft driver but sure enough even though we had unlimited data my phone barely got 2G. I went in the next day and my dad told me to just buy what they had in store ON DISPLAY. The women their didn't seem to know much about what they were selling in that store and I had to research the specs myself. I saw they had the moto e5 plus and liked it for the price cause it's screen was tough, gorilla glass. They did not have it in stock. They began calling other stores to find one and the closest one was in Madison Heights. Before I left I asked the lady what I would need even though I had access to this account and was told all I needed was my driver's license. She failed to mention the tax needed to be paid upfront and I was broke at the time. I didn't find this out until I got there though and the store only had one in stock so I called my dad and we asked them if we could pay it with credit card over the phone and they said no he would have to physically drive there too. My dad then asked me to ask for a manager and I made a joke over the phone to him referring to an old Chappelle show sketch that this guy probably is the manager(I WAS RIGHT) and they weren't going to help didn't even curse. I was then told by this manager to leave his store or he will call the cops and refused to sell me the phone. I then went back to Rochester and my dad just purchased a different phone altogether even though it wasn't what I wanted I needed to work so we put that in the plan and it raised his monthly payments by ten dollars. I called customer support 800 number the next day and explained to a manager, Rose, how horrible we were treated when trying to sign up with them and she assured me that's not how they do business and she would try to acquire what I wanted to begin with and would ship it to us for all our troubles. She said she would call on a specific day and escalated the case. I was patient but then a week went by and the day she was supposed to call she did not. I called the 800 number again and talked with another manager, Crystal, who told me they have no way to ship that particular phone to me because their stores are independently owned and suggested I go back to the Madison heights store to get the phone. Why would anyone go someplace where they were threatened and probably get nowhere again with I asked but she says that's all she could do. This kind of customer service is typical these days no accountability for anyone's actions. I am going to give this phone to my dad and let him work it out with them and get my own plan with a different carrier. I WILL NEVER BUY ANOTHER THING FROM TMOBILE FOR THE REST OF MY LIFE THEY DO NOT CARE ABOUT THEIR CUSTOMERS AT ALL.

Criminal Safe Haven
T-Mobile, Inc (corp office) located at 12920 SE 38th St. Bellevue, Washington 98006 425-378-4000 thinks they are 'Above The Laws'. I have many telephone numbers for over 25 years and late 2018 one of those numbers was stolen from Level 3 Communications. I immediately notified the FCC and varies companies which included T-Mobile (where the criminal(s) had taken the number). T-Mobile was NOT helpful in fact they treated me like I had committed the crime and was NOT the victim. T-Mobile for weeks ask me for ALL types of documentation including (insisting) on a police report (again to acquire MY belongings). After nearly a month of submitting various documents to T-Mobile and many more phone calls regarding MY property T-Mobile finally released MY number back to me.


Here we are again March 2019 this time 2 of my numbers were stolen from Band Width Communications located at  900 Main Campus Dr S te 500  Raleigh, NC 27606 and T-Mobile is involved again assisting the thieves with their crime(s). Both times T-Mobile stated I gave the criminals my information to comment a crime, headache against myself REALLY....T-Mobile. I ask the public to file ANY legitimate complaint with the FCC (consumercomplaints.fcc.gov) regarding ANY of T-Mobile tactics and now T-Mobile is asking to MERGE\buy Sprint (TERRIBLE idea regulators). But IF T-Mobile receives enough complaints T-Mobile can\will be fined hefty and with the PUBLIC'S help it can come true.

I am so tired of calling t-mobile evert time i call i give my 4 diget code and they say it doesn"t work so then they say let me send u a passcode ,i change my 4 diger code everytime i have to call and they wonder whey it does not work,

We went to see David at T-Mobile in Gilbert (Cooper and Warner).  I had inadvertently made an international call on the wrong phone so I had a rather large bill for that call.
David took care of everything for me - he made a call and my bill was reduced by half; in addition our bill will be $5.00 less than before.  I truly am grateful to David's service and kindness.  Thank you, David.

T-Mobile is not a company I would go out of my way to recommend for someone. Their customer service is TERRIBLE. Customer service reps will blanently tell you that things that are messed up on your account are your own fault. There’s been many a times where your bill could be slightly past due (5-6 days), and they will cut off your service without a warning call or text. Sales Reps in the store will lie about what you are being sold (saying phones are free, on promo, “bundle deals”) when this is not the case at all. And T-Mobile is has the only customer care I have experienced that will flat out tell you that there’s nothing they can do to help you, even over simple things such as a bill being quoted too high. ITS RIDICULOUS. 

The only upside to T-Mobile is that it is cheap, and the service is okay for what you pay for. You run into a lot of dead spots, and get a lot of dropped calls.

It’s a subpar cellular service, that is decent for the price they offer. But overall, it’s their GOD AWFUL customer service that makes this review so bad. If their customer service reps cared half as much as their CEO seems to, they’d probably keep customers.

I was excited to switch over to t Mobile but the fact that I preorder a phone and have yet recieved it is crazy! Even more crazy that they keep telling me next week next week even though its released in stores. I am starting to question switching over at all. This is a horrible way to start!

The chat customer service is good, but the customer service by phone is painful and insulting to anyone with any amount of intelligence. They have their service people say all of theses canned, cheesy lines that they don't need to -- what we want in this day and age is the fastest service possible, not saying a bunch of fake nice things that just take up time. The people I've spoken to are always slow and take a lot longer than they need to. I'll never use their call service again -- chat is WAY faster.

T-Mobile phone customer service is an example of why people hate calling customer service. It's embarrassing. 

T-Mobile itself is fine, no better or worse than other carriers I've used.

Sarah is great! I told her I would give her 5 stars for helping me. I know that sometimes the service sucks for people, but no one ever comes here when there is something good to say, and I did have a great experience. I am not a shill just in a good mood. Sorry for everyone having problems I've been in your shoes. Hope someday you'll be in mine!

Sadly disappointed with Phone carrier T-Mobile.  Was with Verizon and received great service, but it came at a high cost.  
T-Mobile is definitely more affordable, but the service and internet connection sucks.  You don’t have to trust me - try them for yourself. Beware however when it comes to transferring over your files to a new phone or simply to the new service contract, T-Mobile will most likely lose your saved voice messages if your coming from Verizon. Read about it.  Others are experiencing the same issue. 
My family has been with T-Mobile for one week and already we have been unable to use our hot spot, have clear phone conversations and keep all our stored voice messages that T-Mobile promised would not be lost.  
The customer service sucks as well!  the representatives act like they know what your concerns are and what they are doing, but clearly have a problem handling the situation.  They send you to other representatives and you end up going around and around.  

We basically receive no service while at home (Oakland, CA) - we have to use a cellspot. Because T-Mobile is Sub and beneath the quality that Verizon promises and delivered - T-Mobile has to try and compete by sending you a plastic unit that must be hooked up to your electric source which is suppose to help create a better connection to the internet.  It does not!  
The bottom line is that T-Mobile is no better than Metro PCS and cost a lot more! I would be disappointed and unhappy if I was a internet phone company that failed my customers continuously.  I’d want to do something about it. The only good thing I can say at this point while dealing with T-Mobile is that there is no contract. I thought this was for us, but it’s not.  I believe T-Mobile knew they could not deliver quality service and decided not to have to deal with legal matters and not place folks into contracts.  Again it’s all about T-Mobile not the customer!

Again it’s all about T-Mobile not the customer! 

Very dissatisfied, disappointed and hurt.

Tmobile took money out of my account that they was not suppose to and it's going on 3 weeks and I'm still going back and forth about my money. They schedule follow calls and dont call tmobile is terrible you be better off with a cricket dont do metro either they are a sister company of tmobile!!!

One, make sure you review and check what phone tmobile is actually giving you when you purchase it at one of their Tmobile stores. They like to switch it and you pay more. I thought I was buying a REVVl and ended buying an LG and was charged $400.00 for the phone.  I spoke to the manager and they pretty much said I was crap out of luck. Then I pay for insurance.  They sent out a refurbished phone for a replacement for one of my lines and it didn't work, I called to get a new one and they refuse to send out another one stating I max out my insurance plan. First time in 6 years using the insurance service and they denied me after replacing my original phone with a LIKE NEW refurbished phone. Spoke to my TMobile supervisor and he lied saying he would send out a new one and nothing. No notes no phone. I will be switching services. I do only pay $25 per month for my service, but you get what you pay for.

If I could go below one star I would. Simply put the service is below average but we accept that because it saves us $20 a month.

The awful part is once you sign with T-Mobile there is no way to upgrade using a promotion. Unless you wanna just give them your phone. 

I have an IPhone X 256gb and they offered $390 as a promotion to get the new S10+. I would think that would include the trade in value of the phone but it does not. Their trade in value of the phone is $550 but because of this “AMAZING PROMOTION “ they are now offering me a solid $390. Only $160 less than what even they value my phone out. 

T-Mobile needs to truly rethink their upgrades.

NEVER EVER trust them to buy a phone with their stupid trade in promotions! you will never heard back from them. I trade in an iphone for a new iphone xr, they never gave me credit for that phone i sent them. contacting them several times they do not have an answer.

Terrible company!!!   Poor coverage, they did not credit me for cell phones as promised, (they just stole my Verizon phones) , no credit for switching companies as promised, and then harassed me about leaving their company and racked up fake charges. I have been with T mobile since the days of voice stream. Like 16 years.

I am at a loss to understand ccx why I stay with them because, frankly their choice to outsource customer service angers me. We have plenty of US citizens that need work. Most of them would be far easier to understand.

I further object to being lied to. If one more India citizen or Fillipino tries to tell me his name is "Bob" I will vomit.

I hate the computerized process to get to an agent instructing me to say "Yes or No" really? As consumers we just are not bright enough to respond to a question? 

Finally, the coverage is fine like everyone else. Prices are fair.

Unfortunately  my days of "needing" a phone that costs more than a grand are over...$1,000.00 is straight stupidity $2,000 for the new foldable S10 they can keep it.

Greed has consumed them and gone is the company that used to appreciate our business. All that remains is a greedy exploitive ( Do you believe outsourced call centers cost as much as those on American soil?) 

I resent my hard earned money being allocated to utilize 3rd world citizens as their unintelligible, aggravating customer service.

The world is changing and little is for the better. It will not improve until we collectively stand up and say "enough". 

I will gladly drop t mobile for any carrier that will pledge not to outsource customer service. This is not a financial choice . This is me wanting someone with English as a first language that will not parrot everything I say without a pause between any syllable in the entire spiel.

This review is for T-Mobile customer service, and overall service, not the local store, which tried to be helpful.

I got T-Mobile prepaid several months ago after being assured there were no coverage issues in my area, and wanted to try the service before I agreed to a contract plan.  They assured me service was no problem in my area. That was simply not the case. Most days I drop multiple calls, and sometimes I have to drive  a mile or so away from home before I can get a signal. This despite the fact that the coverage map says coverage is good in my area, and so did the sales people.

When I returned to the store to ask for help I was told I should call customer service and ask for a signal booster. I was told they would provide one free of charge. That also was not true. When I called customer service I spoke with someone who did not speak English well, but who made it clear that T-Mobile would only offer help with signal issues if I first signed up for a contract plan, rather than prepaid.

When I asked the rep how he expected me to sign a contract when I wasn't receiving decent service, I was told that if I wanted help, I needed to sign a contract. He admitted that a signal booster could help me, but said they would not send me one, or even sell me one directly. I asked him if he was telling me my only option was to go elsewhere for phone service? He said no, you need to sign a contract...

Well, with the service I'm getting, they can forget that. I bought a signal booster from a friend who wasn't using theirs. It has helped a little. Not very much. It is operating perfectly according to its instructions, but it also confirms that quite often my home simply isn't receiving enough signal for it to improve.

I'm really disappointed. I had hoped to be done with looking for phone carriers. I've never had a phone company act like you don't even matter. But I'm glad I only opted for prepaid, so I can at least leave these guys. My advice - if you are considering T-Mobile, make sure you try prepaid BEFORE you sign on for anything. Better yet, try someone else.

I'm not normally one to rant but T-mobile has taken me to my limits. We recently switched from T-mobile to AT&T since they provide internet in the new house we are going to be moving into. Before we switched I requested that T-mobile unlock our phones so my son could use one of the old ones. You have to wait 48 hours for an email and another 48 hours before you can actually unlock the device. When I recieved the email it was for the wrong device and had to request the process again for the right device. By the time I recieved the new email it said they could not unlock it because I no longer had an active account and that my last bill had not processed payment. I then waited for the final payment to process and requested the process all over again with the same response email being recieved 48 hours later. I have now tried 7 times over a span of 20 days to get a device that I own unlocked so I can use it with another carrier. Several times I have been told that a manager would call me back as soon as they get off another line and never did, so I had to call back once again. I spoke to them one last time a few minutes ago and now I am supposedely on a magical "White List" to ensure my order is processed properly this time. 20 days and 7 calls seems to be the magic number to get you put on a list where they will actually do their job...if this even  works. I have to wait 3 more days for it to process, then wait another 48 hours for the email, and finally another 48 to try and unlock the phone. WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE EVER!!!

I was a T-Mobile Customer for more than ten years... we live in an area with poor service, so we recently switched to Cricket.  T-Mobile had loaned us a cellular hotspot for the last few years because we were in another area with poor service.  Now on Cricket, T-Mobile said that we need to return their hotspot... we drove an hour to a T-Mobile store where we returned the device to Leo (store manager Blue Bonnet Store in Baton Rouge, LA).  4 days later, we received a call from a collections company saying we owe for those devices.  Having already returned them, we called customer support.  While I was on the phone with customer support, my wife called the actual store... where the manager (Leo) was standing next to the devices.  Support told us that we were supposed to mail them in, not (like the literature actually instructed) return them to the store.  They said that they can't take us from collections until they have received the devices (which had been in a store for 4 days)... I asked to speak with a manager (so now, we're on the phone with Liz and Leo... two different managers in two different locations)... Liz explains that they can't talk to each other and our only option is to drive an hour to the store to have them verify an ID and the equipment... I explained about the drive... to which she seemed to be unaffected... after more than an hour on the phone, she said she could talk to the manager, but when presented with that opportunity, refused.

I've loved being with T-Mobile for more than ten years... but will hesitate to deal with them again... or recommend them.  Just be careful.

I have been a T-mobile customer for MANY years...close to 20. Things have always been acceptable to me, a bit pricey, but everything always worked pretty well. I recently upgraded, and joined a 55+ program. That gives you a $20.00 break for being 55+, with a few strings attached. I already was doing what I needed to do to get the discount. Here's my complaint...They were able to fit my new equipment ($21.25 a month) into the billing cycle, but not my discount. Therefore they got $20.00 from me instead of $1.95. I don't know how corporate America sleeps at night. You can have the $20.00, but instead of a "great" review you get an "average" review. Sooner or later Karma will get you. Look at Sears with their predatory credit cards as an example. You should reward loyalty. I'm not somebody that lives and dies with my phone. I rate them as average. Nothing to rave about, and not much to complain about.

I would give T-Mobile zero stars if I could.  I was with Verizon for 14 years with excellent coverage.  A bit pricey, so T-Mobile drew me in with their plan less than half of what I paid before.  They said we could keep our devices and pay them off.  Oh no no no.  I found out that you are required to turn in your devices which they would give you credit on, buy new ones through them adding to your bill, and then send you a gift card for the balance after credit of the turned-in phone up to $650.  They also don't mention that you have to fill out a form, provide a receipt from the carrier of the balance, turn the phone(s) into the T-Mobile store within 30 days, and await a gift card to pay the previous carrier which takes 8-10 weeks.  I am now stuck with a $2,700 Verizon bill because they didn't convey any of this.  Luckily I have found Spectrum Mobile to relieve me of the T-Mobile malpractice, but it comes with a price.  I canceled service with them two days after the beginning of their billing cycle.  Should have a prorated credit, right?  Oh, no no no again.  Even though you no longer have any service with them, they still charge you for the entire billing cycle.  The customer service supervisor confirmed and would not relent.  Since it is paid in advance, there is no recourse without legal counsel.  I will never, ever do business with them and discourage anyone from using them.  Please avoid them at all costs.  They are deceptive and unethical.  Not to mention, even though the acquired Sprint towers, their coverage sucks.

In a nutshell, it's not their problem I was overcharged and that I'll need to dispute the charge with my bank. WOW YOU GUYS SHOULD BE EMBARRASSED. It's 2019 and your employees have no guest relations or customer etiquette. I will be telling all my coworkers, friends, family, everyone I come into acquaintance with to drop you. Oh and thank you very much, when asking for a supervisor or manager, I was just placed on hold for another 20 minutes. Real great. You did it again T-Mobile!

I went to a T-Mobile store to move from a pre paid wireless plan to a post paid wireless plan on Saturday 1/19. My husband and I's number needed to be ported to our new post paid account. The team at the T-Mobile store were absolutely amazing and helpful. However, there was some difficulty porting our number. The associate at the store was transferred numerous times and put in a very difficult position. We were assured the numbers would be ported by a representative. They said it could take up to 24 hours. Fast forward to today 1/23. The numbers still have not ported over. My husbands number is still active on the prepaid account yet mine was deactivated. We have contacted T-Mobile at least 6 times to get this issue resolved. All customer service reps were very kind, however none of them were able to find a resolution. Numerous tickets were placed for the software team and no resolution has been found. We were just told "there is no eta on a fix, but it will be fixed." This is absolutely unacceptable that I cannot use my phone or phone number. I utilize my number for my own business. I am losing money daily as I cannot receive my clients calls. I understand that it can take time for a port, but this is ridiculous. Even worse, it seems this is a well known problem that has not been corrected. I expect this to be fixed as soon as possible and my number active again within the next 24 hours.
Excellent customer service and in store customer rep. Store at 871 E Commercial Blvd Oakland Park FL 33334.

I’m a long-term customer who’d leave T-Mobile if I could because their recent customer service is beyond horrible. Upgrading to a plan with more data has been nothing but a headache. Was in the T-Mobile store over an hour just to get that done, for some reason they had to port one of the numbers even though I was only upgrading the plan, so that number didn’t work for hours and I had to call customer service twice about that.

They continued billing me for the old plan, aka something I couldn’t possibly have because you can’t have more than one plan for the same number. Had to call customer service two more times about that. Now my service hasn’t worked for days, keep getting messages saying I need to pay the bill when I’ve paid it, they’ve ended up billing me three times for this month’s service (when it doesn’t even work). They’ve also made changes to my account that  I didn’t request or authorize, switching me to a more expensive plan. Funny they can manage that, but can’t get the service working, and also say they can’t switch the plan back while there’s an “open ticket.” Really? The same ticket that was open when they changed the plan without my consent?

I can’t even believe how much of a mess this has turned into. I’m paying for nothing and they won’t give me a refund. I’ve spent hours and hours talking to customer service, DM-ing them, sending screenshots, adjusting settings on my phone and rebooting it. Get a different story from every person I talk to and zero resolution. Still have no service and they still have my money.

I’m disabled and on a fixed, extremely limited income, so these billing issues are seriously stressful and killing my meager budget. I had to scrape up money to get service activated on an old phone with another carrier just so I could have some sort of service, so that’s even more T-Mobile cost me.

The other carrier? I was in and out of the store within ten minutes with fully functional service, even though I hadn’t been a customer of theirs and they had none of my info on file. Dealing with them was so easy and stress-free in comparison, and the service works better even with an old, outdated phone.

If only I could just switch to them altogether right now...but thanks to T-Mobile keeping my money for service they aren’t providing, I can’t afford to make a full switch. Avoid T-Mobile. At this point, I regret ever being a customer of theirs.
I had T mobile for Years but this last year the service sucks I will move to att or other carrier. Customer service used to be awsome. Now after 12 calls they haven’t resolve a stupid issue.

Tmobile is as shady as they get. Will not us their service again.

I have to send a story to a consumer protection to investigate now. Think twice before subscribe their services.
